item.title: A possible correction:
item.content:encoded:
The line

I kept telling her it wasn’t her fault but It’s like, it isn’t stick, ya?

doesn't make sense to me. The mid-sentence capitalization is not present in the character's speech elsewhere, and "it isn't stick" is an unfamiliar slang expression. It seems to me like these are typos, and the sentence should read:

I kept telling her it wasn’t her fault but It’sit's like, it isn’tdidn't stick, ya?

This feels like too substantial a change to make under the umbrella of spelling/grammar/punctuation, so I'm noting it here for the author to consider.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The use of █████'s in the opening was quite excessive, almost stopped reading. The letters were amazing however, and I especially loved the repetition with each final line. Plus ditto Creathian.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
fuckin'… ninjas cutting onions up in here…

damn good story, can't recommend enough. the buildup and execution was fantastic. as soon as I'm done wiping my nose i'll give it a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love this, no pun intended. You told a wonderful story with the format, and no one's quite mentioned the ending. I like how you kept the identity of the recipient a mystery, and the fact that there ever was one, until the very last dropdown menu. It would of been so easy to add a "To…" at the beginning of the letter, formatting it like a normal letter. The lack of that really increases the punch of this story.

Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a definite improvement over what I read in chat, the added context gave it a lot more emotional impact. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really like how this is mainly a story, and not much focus on the anomaly part of this; like give more hints as to how it was made anomalous, if she wasn't, how did Abigail's letter do this?

That being said, it's a damn good story, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Using the SCP format to tell a story. And an affecting, moving story at that. This is part of what keeps me coming back.

item.title: Nice
item.content:encoded: Holy hell +1 for telling an awesome story!

item.title: You're in the positives!
item.content:encoded:
I can't say I love this enough- you're in the positives and doing great ^^

+1 for ripping my heart out, keep it up Serkov!

item.title: The Last Love Letter
item.content:encoded: Hope this SCP is good enough for'ya all. Thanks to everyone on Site19 for the crit, let's see if this stays afloat eh?
